title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 632.525 - Abrogation of case law, sexually violent offense definition
text:
632.525.  Abrogation of case law, sexually violent offense definition. — It is the intent of the legislature to reject and abrogate earlier case law interpretations on the meaning of or definition of "sexually violent offense" to include, but not be limited to, holdings in: Robertson v. State, 392 S.W.3d 1 (Mo. App. W.D., 2012); and State ex rel.  Whitaker v. Satterfield, 386 S.W.3d 893 (Mo. App. S.D., 2012); and all cases citing, interpreting, applying, or following those cases.  It is the intent of the legislature to apply these provisions retroactively.
(L. 2013 H.B. 215 §1 merged with H.B. 374 & 434 § 1)
